description_long: This futuristic airplane features a sleek, angular fuselage with a dark metallic finish and sharp, faceted surfaces. Its most distinctive elements are the two large, hexagonal ducted fan engines mounted on short, angled pylons extending from either side of the main body, suggesting vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) capability. The cockpit is centrally located and appears to be fully enclosed with a tinted canopy, offering a panoramic forward view. Several thin antennae or sensor masts protrude from the top, adding to its high-tech appearance. The rear of the craft is compact, with visible exhaust ports and subtle blue lighting accents. The overall design is compact and agile, optimized for maneuverability in urban environments.
description: A sleek, dark, angular airplane with twin hexagonal ducted fan engines and a central enclosed cockpit, designed for agile urban flight.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, central cockpit, and prominent hexagonal ducted fans on each side.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the faceted fuselage, angled pylons, and the depth of the ducted fan engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the compact tail section, dual exhaust ports, and blue lighting accents.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the fan structure and antennae.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: No traditional wings; features short, angled pylons with large hexagonal ducted fans in a high-mounted configuration, lacking conventional winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin electric ducted fans mounted on pylons extending from the fuselage sides.
attribute_tail_design: Minimalist tail with no visible vertical or horizontal stabilizers; compact rear section with integrated exhaust ports.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear present in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, fully enclosed cockpit with a single canopy and panoramic window design.
